Requirement: An exception with an empty message must never be treated as a successful run.
Test Setup: None - covered directly by unit tests.
Test Description: uv run pytest discussion_moderation/tests/test_run_results_store.py
Expected Result: normalize_error() and build_models_from_records() never let error='' read as success.
Comments: 5 automated tests exist; confirmed to fail against the pre-fix code, so they genuinely catch the bug.

Requirement: HTTP Basic Auth must protect the dashboard and API (ADR 0043).
Test Setup: None - covered directly by unit tests. Also needs manual verification against the real idril/EC2 servers.
Test Description: uv run pytest discussion_moderation/tests/test_auth.py, then manually check idril/EC2 reject unauthenticated requests.
Expected Result: Unauthenticated requests rejected; correct credentials accepted; /health stays public.
Comments: 8 tests exist at the TestClient level; never verified against the real deployed servers specifically.

Requirement: A live LMS run must handle JWT expiry gracefully, not fail silently or generically.
Test Setup: A JWT close to or past its 1-day expiry.
Test Description: Run a live multi-thread comparison mid-expiry, observe per-thread error reporting.
Expected Result: Clear auth error surfaced per-thread, not a generic pipeline-error misattribution.
Comments: Basic (non-expiry) JWT auth was verified via curl earlier in the project; the expiry case specifically was never exercised.

Requirement: S3 and filesystem run-result backends must produce equivalent output for the same run.
Test Setup: The same run data saved through both backends.
Test Description: Compare EvalRunDetail/EvalThreadResultView output between FilesystemRunResultStore and S3RunResultStore.
Expected Result: No field only populated correctly on one backend.
Comments: Both backends are individually well-tested; nothing compares them to each other yet.

Requirement: Interrupted-run marking must only fire on deployed environments, never during local dev/testing.
Test Setup: Two service starts: one with FACILITATION_ENV unset, one with FACILITATION_ENV=idril.
Test Description: Start the service both ways, check whether docs/experiments/results/ files get mutated.
Expected Result: Local start: no files changed. idril-env start: stale runs correctly marked interrupted.
Comments: No automated test exists for this specific guard.

Requirement: The Moderator's flag_content tool must reach a real backend implementation without crashing.
Test Setup: None - covered directly by a regression test.
Test Description: uv run pytest discussion_moderation/tests/test_agents_wiring.py::test_moderator_flag_content_tool_reaches_backend
Expected Result: Tool call succeeds against LMSBackend/OpenEdXBackend/StubLMSBackend.
Comments: Was a real AttributeError bug (method didn't exist on any backend), fixed this session. Confirmed the test fails with the original error against the pre-fix code.

Requirement: The pipeline's graph control flow (node routing, retry loop) must behave correctly, verified fast and deterministically.
Test Setup: Needs writing: a pydantic-ai TestModel-based test of facilitation_graph/run_pipeline directly (doesn't exist).
Test Description: Write and run a test covering OrchestratorNode->RoleNode routing, the rule-check retry loop, and the End-without-response fallback.
Expected Result: All three behaviors confirmed under a deterministic model, no real LLM call needed.
Comments: Currently only exercised via slow, non-deterministic real-LLM eval runs. Feeds into feasibility-tests.csv A09 (graceful degradation), which needs this same harness for fault injection.

Requirement: Structural response assertions (ADR 0020 Layer 1) must be directly unit tested, not just exercised implicitly.
Test Setup: Needs writing: direct tests for assert_facilitation_response.
Test Description: Write tests for each invariant (non-empty text, known technique, confidence range, post_to_thread coherence), including failure cases.
Expected Result: Each invariant has a passing and a failing test case.

Requirement: A live LMS run in dry-run mode (FACILITATION_BOT_USER_ID unset) must never actually post to the thread, while still returning an accurate result.
Test Setup: Service started with FACILITATION_BOT_USER_ID unset (ADR 0030).
Test Description: Run the full pipeline against a live or stub thread with the bot user ID unset, then check whether post_comment was called.
Expected Result: post_comment is never invoked; PipelineResult still returns comment_posted=false and the generated text.
Comments: New from ADR 0030 review (2026-07-20). Distinct from Q02/A13's live-posting path, which assumes the bot user ID is set.

Requirement: POST /facilitate must time out cleanly, not hang the process, when the underlying pipeline call stalls.
Test Setup: An artificially slow/unreachable model (or a deliberately short pipeline_timeout_seconds).
Test Description: Inject a slow LLM call and confirm the REST layer returns 504 within pipeline_timeout_seconds.
Expected Result: 504 returned; process keeps serving other requests.
Comments: New from ADR 0026 review (2026-07-20). No existing row covers REST-layer timeout handling specifically.

Requirement: The eval runner's rate-limit retry must not fire on unrelated errors whose message happens to contain '429', 'rate', or 'quota'.
Test Setup: A mocked validation error whose message text coincidentally contains one of those substrings.
Test Description: Construct such an error and run it through the runner's retry-classification logic.
Expected Result: Runner does not misclassify it as a rate-limit error and does not incorrectly retry (or the false-positive is confirmed and logged as a known risk, per ADR 0027).
Comments: New from ADR 0027 review (2026-07-20) - ADR explicitly names this as a documented risk of string-matching on exception messages.

Requirement: Model-profile resolution must follow its documented precedence order (runtime env override > MODEL_PROFILES entry > provider default > base ModelProfile()).
Test Setup: Same model name configured differently at each of the four levels.
Test Description: Unit test asserting the override wins, then remove each level in turn to confirm correct fallthrough.
Expected Result: Resolution always picks the highest-precedence level that's populated.
Comments: New from ADR 0031 review (2026-07-20).

Requirement: The EC2-deployed OAuth2 client_credentials JWT must expire in exactly 86400s (JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_SECONDS), not silently revert to a default.
Test Setup: Fresh EC2 deploy with TUTOR_PLUGINS_ROOT exported before tutor config save, per ADR 0040.
Test Description: Curl the token endpoint after a deploy and check expires_in.
Expected Result: expires_in == 86400.
Comments: New from ADR 0040 review (2026-07-20) - this value silently regressed once already per the ADR. Load-bearing for feasibility-tests.csv A13's live end-to-end run, since a wrong TTL would surface as Q03/A13 failures with a misleading root cause.

Requirement: The run history list must reflect live status without a manual refresh.
Test Setup: A run in progress (trigger any fixture/model combination).
Test Description: Watch the Runs list without touching the refresh button for at least 15 seconds.
Expected Result: Status/progress column updates automatically (polls every 3s while a run is active).
Comments: Was broken and fixed this session (list showed a stale snapshot); needs re-confirming after further changes.

Requirement: Cancel must actually stop a run within the pipeline's configured timeout, not hang indefinitely.
Test Setup: A model that's slow or unreachable (or a deliberately short pipeline_timeout_seconds for the test).
Test Description: Trigger a run against it, click Cancel, time how long it takes to reach 'cancelled'.
Expected Result: Transitions to cancelled within one pipeline_timeout_seconds window.
Comments: Root cause (missing asyncio.wait_for) was fixed earlier this project cycle; automated coverage exists at the logic level (test_run_experiment_cancellation.py, mocked) but not through the actual dashboard/HTTP path.

Requirement: Retry must rebuild the exact original run's thread/model selection.
Test Setup: A completed or cancelled run to retry.
Test Description: Open the run detail page, click Retry run, confirm the Trigger form, click Trigger run again.
Expected Result: Same threads/model re-selected; name gets a "(2)" suffix; a second run actually starts.
Comments: A real bug (name silently overwritten by a render-order race) was found and fixed here this session.

Requirement: Retry must not pre-select models/threads that no longer exist in the current environment.
Test Setup: A historical run using a model not in the current environment's EVAL_MODELS.
Test Description: Retry that run and check which checkboxes come pre-selected.
Expected Result: Only threads/models that still exist get checked; nothing errors.

Requirement: A thread that failed must show an error, never be silently counted as a success.
Test Setup: A thread whose pipeline call raises an exception with an empty message (e.g. bare asyncio.TimeoutError).
Test Description: Trigger such a failure, open the run, check the error banner and the error_count/completion_count.
Expected Result: Error banner shown; counted as an error, not a success.
Environment: N/A (also covered by automated pytest, see Q01)
Comments: This is the empty-string counting bug fixed this session.

Requirement: Thread content (opening post + full comment tree) must render in the run detail view.
Test Setup: A completed run.
Test Description: Open the model detail view, expand a thread row.
Expected Result: Full opening post and threaded replies with author names render; truncated preview in the collapsed row.
Comments: Backend gap (thread_body/thread_comments were never persisted) fixed this project cycle.

Requirement: The landing page must show accurate environment health and handle a dead target gracefully.
Test Setup: One environment (idril or EC2) target made temporarily unreachable.
Test Description: Load docs/index.html, observe the status dot and the card; try switching environments with a single click.
Expected Result: Status dot reflects checking/online/offline correctly; offline notice instead of a blank iframe; single click switches, no double-click needed.

Requirement: Two runs triggered concurrently must not interfere with each other.
Test Setup: None beyond the dashboard itself.
Test Description: Trigger a run, then trigger a second run before the first completes; watch both in the list and their own detail views.
Expected Result: Both update independently; cancelling one doesn't affect the other.
Comments: Never tested. Expected to work since polling is keyed by run ID, but that's an assumption.

Requirement: A fetch failure for one thread's intervention history must not break the display of other threads in the same view.
Test Setup: One thread's history endpoint made to fail (mock or kill the underlying store mid-request).
Test Description: Load ModelDetail with multiple threads and force thread A's history fetch to fail.
Expected Result: Thread A shows a local error state; thread B and others render their history normally.
Comments: New from ADR 0036 review (2026-07-20). Distinct from Q18/Q19 - this is cross-thread error isolation within one view.

Requirement: A committed, repeatable automated test suite should exist for the dashboard.
Test Setup: N/A - this is a structural gap, not a single test.
Test Description: Check dashboard/package.json for a test script, and dashboard/src for *.test.*/*.spec.* files.
Expected Result: A vitest/Testing Library suite for components, and a small committed Playwright suite for the flows above.
Comments: Every row above currently relies on one-off manual verification, not CI-enforced regression protection.

Requirement: The same fixture must behave consistently across environments before it's relied on live (demo risk, not a research claim).
Test Setup: Same model where possible, on two different environments.
Test Description: Run the `dominated` fixture on local and on one of idril/EC2.
Expected Result: Both environments complete cleanly.
Comments: Do this shortly before any live demo - environment drift is possible, this is a sanity check not a one-time fact. Moved here (not feasibility-tests.csv) because you flagged this in the sheet's own original comment as demo risk, not a research claim.
